text.title: argocd-gitops Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for argocd-gitops. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Never create Applications manually in the UI for production — manage all Application manifests in Git (App of Apps or ApplicationSet pattern).
rule.rationale: Manually created Applications cannot be version-controlled or reviewed. A deleted ArgoCD instance with manual Applications means starting from scratch. Git as the single source of truth applies to ArgoCD's own configuration, not just application deployments.

rule.statement: Enable selfHeal: true on all environments to prevent configuration drift; use syncWindows to restrict automated syncs to maintenance windows for production.
rule.rationale: Without self-heal, a manual kubectl apply in production creates drift that is invisible until the next sync. Self-heal corrects this within seconds. syncWindows prevent ArgoCD from syncing production during incidents by restricting automated syncs to designated maintenance windows (e.g., 2-4 AM UTC).

rule.statement: Use folders to model environments, not branches — one repository with overlays/ for each environment is simpler than branch-per-environment.
rule.rationale: Branch-per-environment requires cherry-picking changes across branches and makes diff between environments difficult. Folder-per-environment shows environment differences as a clear Kustomize overlay diff. Operators can understand production setup by reading the production overlay without merging branches mentally.

rule.statement: Create an AppProject per team with explicit sourceRepos, destinations, and roles — do not deploy all applications to the default project.
rule.rationale: The default project has no restrictions. An AppProject scopes a team to their repos and namespaces, preventing accidental or malicious cross-team deployments. A misconfigured Application owned by team-A cannot deploy to team-B's namespace because the Project boundary forbids it.

rule.statement: Use sync-wave annotations to order resource creation: namespaces and CRDs at wave -1, ConfigMaps and Secrets at wave 0, Deployments at wave 1, Ingress at wave 2.
rule.rationale: Dependency ordering prevents transient sync failures. A CRD must exist before a Custom Resource that uses it. A ConfigMap must be created before a Deployment that mounts it.

rule.statement: Do not store Kubernetes Secrets directly in the GitOps repository. Use External Secrets Operator, Sealed Secrets, or Vault integration instead.
rule.rationale: Git is not a secure secret store. Use a dedicated secrets backend (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, etc.). External Secrets Operator syncs secrets from the backend into the cluster; Sealed Secrets encrypts secrets in Git so only the cluster can decrypt them.

rule.statement: Configure notifications for on-sync-failed and on-health-degraded triggers at minimum; send to both Slack and a ticketing system for production.
rule.rationale: Sync failures without notification go undetected. ArgoCD self-heals configuration drift but cannot fix broken images or missing dependencies — human alert is required. Production deployments must have an audit trail (on-deployed notifications with Git commit info).

rule.statement: Keep revisionHistoryLimit: 5+ in Applications to enable easy rollback. A rollback is a Git revert + ArgoCD sync, not a manual re-deployment.
rule.rationale: ArgoCD tracks the last N revisions (commits) that were synced. Rollback is selecting a previous revision and syncing. This is declarative and audited in Git.

rule.statement: Apply ArgoCD config to one cluster / control plane per pass. Skip this methodology entirely when (a) target is non-Kubernetes, (b) single-developer project where kubectl apply is sufficient, or (c) air-gapped environments without Git mirror.
rule.rationale: Anti-conflation: cluster-bound configs reference cluster-specific Application + AppProject names; cross-cluster passes leak naming.
